profile.name: Walt Jr. (Flynn) White
profile.headline: Driven high school student with a passion for breakfast cuisine, digital media, and advocacy for people with disabilities.

education.coursework: English Literature, U.S. History, Biology, Algebra II, Web Design, Speech & Debate

activities.name: SaveWalterWhite.com
activities.description: Created and managed a fundraising website that went viral, handling donations, public messaging, and media inquiries. Learned web publishing, payment processing, and crisis communications in real time.

activities.name: Disability Advocacy
activities.description: Informal peer advocate for students with cerebral palsy and other physical disabilities. Navigated accessibility challenges daily and helped normalize disability in school social circles.

activities.name: Family Caregiving
activities.description: Took on significant household responsibilities during family health crisis. Managed logistics, provided emotional support to younger sibling, and maintained academic performance under extreme stress.

explorerData.driveStatement: I want to do something that matters. I've been through stuff most people my age haven't, and it made me tougher. I don't want to just get a job — I want to build something, maybe in food or media or something where I can be my own person and not just 'Walter White's kid.'

careerPaths.title: Digital Marketing & Social Media Manager
careerPaths.whyFit: Your experience creating SaveWalterWhite.com shows natural instincts for digital storytelling, audience engagement, and campaign management. You built a viral fundraising platform with zero training — that's exactly what this field values.
nextSteps: Take free Google Digital Marketing & E-commerce certificate
Start a food blog or Instagram account reviewing breakfast spots in Albuquerque
Learn basic analytics with Google Analytics certification
Build a portfolio of 3-4 small campaign projects

careerPaths.title: Restaurant & Hospitality Management
careerPaths.whyFit: Your deep passion for breakfast culture and food, combined with your resilience and people skills, make hospitality a strong fit. The industry values grit and personality over credentials, and your advocacy experience translates directly to inclusive customer service.
careerPaths.growth: Moderate — 10% projected growth, strong in metro areas
nextSteps: Get a part-time job at a local restaurant to learn operations
Research culinary programs at Central New Mexico Community College
Study for ServSafe Food Handler certification
Visit local restaurant owners and ask about their career paths

skillsToLearn.name: Team Leadership
skillsToLearn.how: Shift lead experience → assistant manager track

careerPaths.title: Disability Services Coordinator
careerPaths.whyFit: Living with cerebral palsy gives you authentic, irreplaceable perspective on accessibility and disability services. Your self-advocacy and peer support experience is exactly what universities, nonprofits, and corporations need in their disability services programs.
careerPaths.growth: Strong — growing demand for DEI and accessibility roles
nextSteps: Look into Disability Studies programs at University of New Mexico
Connect with the New Mexico Governor's Commission on Disability
Volunteer with a local disability advocacy organization
Get certified in ADA compliance basics

skillsToLearn.name: Grant Writing
skillsToLearn.how: Coursera Grant Writing course + practice applications

careerPaths.title: Web Developer & UX Designer
careerPaths.whyFit: You taught yourself enough web development to build and launch a functional website with payment processing. With formal training, your hands-on experience and natural understanding of accessibility (from personal experience) would make you valuable in UX design, especially for inclusive digital products.
nextSteps: Complete freeCodeCamp's Responsive Web Design certification
Learn accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) — your lived experience is an advantage
Build 3 portfolio projects focusing on accessible design
Look into UNM's Computer Science or Web Development programs

skillsToLearn.name: UI/UX Design & Figma
skillsToLearn.how: Google UX Design Certificate on Coursera

skillsToLearn.name: WCAG Accessibility Standards
skillsToLearn.how: W3C Web Accessibility course (free) + IAAP certification

careerPaths.title: Communications & Public Relations Specialist
careerPaths.whyFit: Managing public communications during your family's crisis — media inquiries, public perception, emotional messaging — gave you real-world PR experience most entry-level candidates don't have. You understand how to craft a narrative under pressure.
careerPaths.growth: Moderate — 6% projected growth, versatile across industries
nextSteps: Study Communications or Journalism at UNM
Join the school newspaper or start a podcast
Take a public speaking course or join Toastmasters
Build a writing portfolio with opinion pieces on topics you care about

skillsToLearn.name: Media Relations & Press Writing
skillsToLearn.how: PRSA student membership + press release workshops

evidence.outcome: Built SaveWalterWhite.com from scratch — a fundraising website that went viral
evidence.description: Self-taught web publishing to create a functional website with donation processing, compelling copy, and media-ready content during a family health crisis

evidence.outcome: Managed viral fundraising campaign that attracted national attention and significant donations
evidence.description: Handled donation flow, donor communications, and public messaging for SaveWalterWhite.com

evidence.outcome: Navigated media inquiries and public attention during family health emergency
evidence.description: Crafted public-facing messaging while protecting family privacy during an intensely personal crisis

evidence.outcome: Consistently navigated accessibility challenges in a school and community not designed for physical disabilities
evidence.description: Daily self-advocacy for accommodations, access, and inclusion while maintaining dignity and peer relationships

evidence.outcome: Maintained 3.2 GPA and household stability through family crisis involving father's cancer diagnosis and treatment
evidence.description: Balanced academic performance, family caregiving for younger sibling, and emotional processing of radical family changes

evidence.outcome: Wrote compelling website copy that resonated with thousands of visitors and drove donations
evidence.description: Created authentic, emotionally engaging content for fundraising site without formal writing training

evidence.outcome: Served as informal peer advocate for students with disabilities at J.P. Wynne High School
evidence.description: Helped normalize disability in school social circles and supported peers navigating similar accessibility challenges

evidence.outcome: Set up functional website with integrated payment processing
evidence.description: Learned HTML, basic site configuration, and payment gateway integration through self-directed learning

evidence.outcome: Managed online engagement and audience growth for viral fundraising campaign
evidence.description: Responded to comments, managed public perception, and maintained consistent messaging across channels

evidence.outcome: Navigated complex family dynamics during father's illness with unusual maturity
evidence.description: Processed betrayal, grief, anger, and reconciliation while supporting mother and younger sister through the same crisis

evidence.outcome: Repeatedly adjusted to major life disruptions — father's diagnosis, family upheaval, identity questions — while staying academically and socially on track
evidence.description: Demonstrated consistent ability to recalibrate expectations and find new footing when the ground shifted

evidence.outcome: Developing expertise in breakfast cuisine through enthusiastic daily practice and study
evidence.description: Known among family and friends for breakfast passion — the kind of authentic food obsession that restaurant careers are built on

evidence.outcome: Lived experience navigating physical spaces, technology, and social environments with cerebral palsy
evidence.description: Firsthand understanding of accessibility barriers, ADA compliance gaps, and what inclusive design actually means in practice

evidence.outcome: Managed donor and visitor interactions on fundraising website
evidence.description: Responded to inquiries, handled sensitive questions, and maintained professional tone in high-stress public-facing role

evidence.outcome: Set up and managed online donation processing system
evidence.description: Configured payment gateway, tracked donations, and ensured funds were properly received and accounted for

purpose: To build something of my own — not defined by my family's story or my disability, but by what I choose to create. I want to prove that where you start doesn't determine where you end up.
